author: Earthbux
title: Why methane is the overlooked climate lever
body: Carbon dioxide gets all the headlines, but methane is 80x more potent over 20 years. A new wave of detection satellites is finally giving us the data to act.

author: terra_watcher
body: The Carbon Capture Expansion proposal sounds promising, but I'd want to see more detail on where the captured carbon actually goes. Underground sequestration has a patchy record.

author: CarbonBridge Foundation
title: Our Q1 capture report is live
body: We processed 1,240 tonnes of CO₂ across our three pilot sites in Q1. Full methodology and third-party verification linked below.

author: Earthbux
title: The seabed gold rush no one is talking about
body: Deep sea mining permits are being issued faster than the science can keep up. Here's what we know about the ecosystems at risk — and who's pushing for extraction.

author: reef_diver_92
body: Coral Restoration Alliance is doing work I believe in. Watched ReefGen's nursery operation in person last year — the transplant survival rates were genuinely impressive.

author: Earthbux
title: Soil is the carbon sink we keep ignoring
body: Healthy agricultural soil can sequester billions of tonnes of carbon annually. The problem is we've spent 70 years depleting it. Regenerative farming is not optional — it's arithmetic.

author: savannahsoil
body: The Indigenous Land Title Fund should be the top priority here. Without secure land rights, every other land initiative is built on sand. Organizations can leave; communities stay.

author: Earthbux
title: The Amazon's new defenders are wearing headphones
body: Acoustic monitoring is changing how indigenous rangers detect illegal activity. The sound of a chainsaw carries half a kilometer. A $200 sensor can hear it.

author: Wildlands Network EU
title: Corridor survey complete in the Carpathians
body: Our field teams have mapped 340km of viable wolf and lynx migration routes between the Slovak and Polish Carpathians. Full report submitted to the EU rewilding registry.

author: Earthbux
title: Bees are running out of road
body: Intensive agriculture has eliminated 97% of wildflower meadows in the UK since the 1930s. Corridor planting along transport infrastructure is one of the few scalable interventions left.

author: migueld_env
body: Climate displacement is the fastest-growing category of forced migration and almost nobody in the legal world is treating it seriously yet. This fund fills a real gap.

author: Earthbux
title: The village that built its own grid
body: A cooperative in rural Senegal now generates more power than it uses and sells the surplus back to the national grid. GridSpark helped. Here's the full story.

author: AquaCommons
title: System 14 is online in Bihar
body: Our 14th community water system serves 4,200 people in the Muzaffarpur district. Average daily collection time dropped from 3.1 hours to 8 minutes.
